creator: k@numeri.campus.luth.se
text:
------- Forwarded Message
From: Kevin Day <toasty@dragondata.com>
Message-Id: <200008112108.QAA87708@celery.dragondata.com>
Subject: Re: kern/8657: nfs client hung in nfs_bwrite/vfs_busy_pages
To: johan@FreeBSD.org
Date: Fri, 11 Aug 2000 16:08:54 -0500 (CDT)
Cc: toasty@dragondata.com, dillon@FreeBSD.org
In-Reply-To: <200008101541.IAA02686@freefall.freebsd.org> from "johan@FreeBSD.org" at Aug 10, 2000 08:41:51 AM
X-Mailer: ELM [version 2.5 PL3]
MIME-Version: 1.0
Content-Type: text/plain; charset=us-ascii
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit
Sender: toasty@celery.dragondata.com

> 
> Synopsis: nfs client hung in nfs_bwrite/vfs_busy_pages
> 
> State-Changed-From-To: open->feedback
> State-Changed-By: johan
> State-Changed-When: Thu Aug 10 08:36:41 PDT 2000
> State-Changed-Why: 
> Kevin,
> Alot has happened in the NFS area, have you seen this
> in a newer release of FreeBSD, for example 4.1-Release.
>  
> 
> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=8657
> 

I haven't seen this on any recent systems, but it does still exist in 2.2.8.

Feel free to close it.

- -- Kevin


------- End of Forwarded Message
